acceptance_criteria: docs/GATE-M3.md records M3 Roadmap and acceptance-matrix evidence for polity/vassalage obligations, administrative burden, 50-80 characters/relationships/offices, appointment/enfeoffment/policy workflows, tribute/troop fulfillment, postwar arrangements, minimal succession, appointment/bulk/reason UI, H-003/H-005 initial validation, and historical claim pipeline readiness.
M3 exit evidence proves three postwar governance methods produce different 24-month consequences.
M3 exit evidence proves lord/office holder changes preserve jurisdiction policy while changing execution performance.
M3 exit evidence proves suzerain chains are acyclic and obligations are auditable without duplicate fulfillment.
M3 exit evidence proves character behavior reasons are visible through accepted read models/UI.
Historical names, titles, and relationships are either absent from formal content or routed through the accepted claim pipeline with labels and review evidence.
No product code, package code, content-source data, roadmap edits, accepted ADR changes, M4 campaign loop, M5 vertical-slice scope, or frozen-decision changes are modified by this validation task.
systems_architect writer and independent qa_reviewer reviewer produce validated handoffs before lead marks M3 accepted.
required_tests: node -e JSON validation for M3 gate task, goal-mode-state, model-routing-state, relevant M3 handoffs, and systems handoff
M3 scenario command proving three 24-month governance outcomes
M3 client reason UI Playwright or Storybook/browser smoke
notes: MILESTONE-AUTONOMY-AMENDMENT-001 allows ordinary M3 exit only when the acceptance matrix, tests, independent review, and no R4/frozen-decision Human Gate conditions are satisfied.
This task validates M3 only; it must not start M4 campaign, supply, siege, battle, or full diplomacy work.
If H-003/H-005 evidence is inconclusive, record PASS_WITH_LIMITS or BLOCKED rather than claiming M3 exit.
